title: UK AI Security Institute finds open-weight models now trail frontier cyber capability by 4-7 months, down from 6-10
watch: AISI's planned Kimi K3 evaluation, once Kimi K3's weights are publicly released
Any independent critique or walk-back of AISI's methodology
Whether AISI includes Opus 4.7/4.8 in a future comparison round
cycle_2026-07-19_night_c2_note: New standalone story (dedup-check: no match), sourced from cycle 1's Tier-0 sweep. Fresh verifier fetched AISI's primary blog post directly (Tier-0), cross-checked against The Decoder's independent write-up (Tier-2). Fresh adversarial falsifier independently re-fetched AISI's primary text twice, confirmed the DeepSeek-vs-Sonnet-4.5 cyber-range detail is stated verbatim in the primary source (not a secondary-source addition, as the verifier had cautiously flagged), corrected a cost-attribution imprecision ($85 covers both Opus 4.5 and 4.6, not one model), and surfaced a new caveat: AISI's comparison set omits Anthropic's more recent Opus 4.7/4.8. No contradicting reports found. Research-finding claims confirmed against AISI's own primary evaluation; article notes it is a blog post, not peer-reviewed. Style gates run on both languages: pass (no padding, headline falsifiable and supported by confirmed claims, TL;DR standalone, Turkish mirrors structure natively).
